title: Dropbox Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Dropbox** is a cloud file storage + sharing platform exposing a JSON-RPC-style REST API v2 split across two hostnames: `https://api.dropboxapi.com/2` for RPC endpoints (metadata, search, sharing, account) and `https://content.dropboxapi.com/2` for binary content endpoints (`upload`, `download`). This connector — `DropboxConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "dropbox"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Dropbox:

| Surface | Capability |
|---|---|
| Files (RPC) | list_folder + continue, get_metadata, copy/move/delete, create_folder, search_v2, list_revisions, restore |
| Files (content) | upload, download — JSON args in `Dropbox-API-Arg` header, binary in body |
| Sharing | create_shared_link_with_settings, list_shared_links |
| Users | get_current_account, get_account, get_space_usage |
| Auth | OAuth2 authorization code + offline refresh, token_revoke |

The connector normalises Dropbox files + folders into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts) honouring `Retry-After`, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::DropboxHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create a Dropbox app

1. Sign in to https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps.
2. Click **Create app**.
3. Choose **Scoped access**, **Full Dropbox** or **App folder** as appropriate.
4. Name the app (e.g. `Shielva-Production`).
5. On the app **Settings** tab, copy the **App key** (= OAuth2 `client_id`) and **App secret** (= `client_secret`).
6. Under **Permissions**, enable the scopes the connector requires:
   - `files.metadata.read`
   - `files.content.read`
   - `files.content.write`
   - `sharing.read`
   - `sharing.write`
   - `account_info.read`
7. Under **OAuth 2 → Redirect URIs**, register the redirect URI the Shielva gateway will use (e.g. `https://gateway.shielva.local/oauth/callback`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Dropbox**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` | App key from step 5 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | App secret from step 5 |
| `token_url` (optional) | leave blank to use `https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token` |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `client_id` + `client_secret` are present (it does **not** call the API yet) and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)` with `requires_oauth_redirect: true`. The gateway then opens the Dropbox consent popup pointing at `https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize?...&token_access_type=offline`.

### 3. Authorize

After the user approves, Dropbox redirects to the registered URI with `?code=...`. The gateway calls `authorize(auth_code, state)` which POSTs to `https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token` with `grant_type=authorization_code`. On success the connector stores a `TokenInfo` carrying both `access_token` (4-hour TTL) and `refresh_token`.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which POSTs `/2/users/get_current_account` as a lightweight probe. On 200 → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED` (with `email`, `display_name` in metadata). On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Dropbox uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`). Access tokens are short-lived (4 hours); a **refresh token** is issued at first authorize because we request `token_access_type=offline` and is used by `on_token_refresh()` to mint new access tokens ahead of expiry.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret` are present. Persists install config. **Does not call the API**. Returns `PENDING` so the gateway opens the consent popup. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | POST `https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token` with `grant_type=authorization_code`, `client_id`, `client_secret`, `code`, `redirect_uri`. Returns `TokenInfo(access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, scopes)`. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | POST the token endpoint with `grant_type=refresh_token`. If Dropbox omits a new `refresh_token` in the response, the prior one is preserved. |
| `health_check()` | POST `/2/users/get_current_account` — `null` body. |
| `disconnect()` | POST `/2/auth/token/revoke`, then `clear_token()`. Auth errors during revoke are swallowed (already invalid → still proceed to local cleanup). |

### Header contract — RPC endpoints

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
```
Empty-body endpoints (`/users/get_current_account`, `/users/get_space_usage`) MUST send the literal JSON value `null` — sending `{}` makes Dropbox return 400.

### Header contract — content endpoints (`upload` / `download`)

```
Authorization:    Bearer <access_token>
Dropbox-API-Arg:  <json-encoded args>          ← JSON moves OUT of body
Content-Type:     application/octet-stream     ← upload only
```

Downloads return the binary payload in the body and the JSON metadata in the `Dropbox-API-Result` header — the connector encodes the body as base64 in `content_b64` and parses the header into `metadata` before returning.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant — read via `self.config.get(...)`)

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth2 client_id (Dropbox App Key) |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth2 client_secret |
| `token_url` | string | no (default) | — | Token endpoint override (autofill default = Dropbox URL) |
| `redirect_uri` | string | injected | `self.config.get("redirect_uri", "")` | Gateway injects before calling `authorize()` |

### Class constants (provider-wide — NOT install_fields)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE        = "dropbox"
AUTH_TYPE             = "oauth2_code"
AUTH_URI              = "https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize"
TOKEN_URI             = "https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token"
REVOKE_URI            = "https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/auth/token/revoke"
REQUIRED_SCOPES       = [
    "files.metadata.read",
    "files.content.read",
    "files.content.write",
    "sharing.read",
    "sharing.write",
    "account_info.read",
]
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS  = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP           = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_API_BASE             = "https://api.dropboxapi.com/2"
_CONTENT_BASE         = "https://content.dropboxapi.com/2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `DropboxConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::DropboxHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`. Does NOT call the API. Returns `HEALTHY + PENDING`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state=None) -> TokenInfo`
Exchange the OAuth code at `TOKEN_URI`. Requires `self.config["redirect_uri"]` (gateway-injected). Stores the token via `set_token()`.

### `async on_token_refresh() -> Optional[TokenInfo]`
Uses the stored refresh token to mint a new access token. Returns `None` when no refresh token is on file.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
POST `/users/get_current_account`. Classifies errors via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Drain `/files/list_folder` (recursive, cursor pagination) → `normalize_entry` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(documents_found / synced / failed)`.

### `async disconnect() -> ConnectorStatus`
POST `/2/auth/token/revoke`, then `clear_token()`. Swallows auth errors so an already-invalid token never blocks cleanup.

children.title: Files (RPC)
children.content:
### `async list_folder(path="", recursive=False, limit=200) -> Dict`
`POST /files/list_folder` — body: `{path, recursive, limit, include_media_info, include_deleted, include_has_explicit_shared_members}`.

### `async list_folder_continue(cursor) -> Dict`
`POST /files/list_folder/continue` — body: `{cursor}`.

### `async get_metadata(path) -> Dict`
`POST /files/get_metadata`. Returns the full file/folder metadata dict.

### `async copy_file(from_path, to_path, autorename=False) -> Dict`
`POST /files/copy_v2` — body: `{from_path, to_path, autorename, allow_shared_folder, allow_ownership_transfer}`.

### `async move_file(from_path, to_path, autorename=False) -> Dict`
`POST /files/move_v2`.

### `async delete_file(path) -> Dict`
`POST /files/delete_v2` — body: `{path}`.

### `async create_folder(path, autorename=False) -> Dict`
`POST /files/create_folder_v2`.

### `async search(query, max_results=100, path="") -> Dict`
`POST /files/search_v2` — body: `{query, options: {max_results, file_status: "active", path}}`.

### `async list_revisions(path, limit=10) -> Dict`
`POST /files/list_revisions` — body: `{path, mode: "path", limit}`.

### `async restore_revision(path, rev) -> Dict`
`POST /files/restore` — body: `{path, rev}`.

children.title: Files (content)
children.content:
### `async download_file(path) -> Dict`
`POST https://content.dropboxapi.com/2/files/download` with args in `Dropbox-API-Arg`. Returns `{metadata, content_b64, size}`.

### `async upload_file(path, content: bytes, mode="add", autorename=False) -> Dict`
`POST https://content.dropboxapi.com/2/files/upload`. `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`. Args (`path, mode, autorename, mute, strict_conflict`) travel in `Dropbox-API-Arg`. Returns the metadata of the uploaded file.

children.title: Sharing
children.content:
### `async create_shared_link(path, settings=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sharing/create_shared_link_with_settings`.

### `async list_shared_links(path=None, cursor=None, direct_only=True) -> Dict`
`POST /sharing/list_shared_links`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async get_current_account() -> Dict`
`POST /users/get_current_account` — body MUST be JSON `null`.

### `async get_account(account_id) -> Dict`
`POST /users/get_account`.

### `async get_space_usage() -> Dict`
`POST /users/get_space_usage` — body MUST be JSON `null`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
All Dropbox HTTP errors are mapped to a typed exception hierarchy rooted at `DropboxError`. The HTTP client owns the mapping; `connector.py` only catches.

| HTTP | Exception | `_STATUS_MAP` classification |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | `DropboxBadRequestError` | (caller-side) |
| 401 | `DropboxAuthError` | `OFFLINE / TOKEN_EXPIRED` |
| 403 | `DropboxAuthError` | `UNHEALTHY / INVALID_CREDENTIALS` |
| 404 | `DropboxNotFoundError` | — |
| 409 (`*/not_found`) | `DropboxNotFoundError` | — |
| 409 (other) | `DropboxConflictError` | — |
| 429 | `DropboxRateLimitError(retry_after_s)` | `DEGRADED / CONNECTED` |
| 5xx | `DropboxServerError` | retry candidate |
| timeout / connection | `DropboxNetworkError` | retry candidate |

### Retry policy (owned by `http_client._request_rpc` / `_request_content`)

- 3 attempts total, exponential backoff `0.5 → 1.0 → 2.0 s`.
- 429 honours the `Retry-After` header, capped at 30 s.
- 5xx + `httpx.TimeoutException` + `httpx.NetworkError` retried.
- 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 raised immediately — never retried.

Dropbox encodes "not found" as HTTP 409 with a `not_found` tag inside the JSON error body — the connector handles this and surfaces it as `DropboxNotFoundError` for predictable downstream branching.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### `expired_access_token/` (401)
The access token has crossed its 4-hour TTL. `BaseConnector.ensure_token()` should have refreshed it; check that `on_token_refresh()` succeeded and that the refresh token is still stored. Re-run authorize() if `refresh_token` is gone.

### 409 `path/not_found/..`
The path you sent does not exist in the user's Dropbox. Dropbox uses 409 (not 404) for missing paths — the connector raises `DropboxNotFoundError`. Verify the path begins with `/` (or is empty for the root).

### 400 `invalid_request` during token exchange
The `redirect_uri` sent to `/oauth2/token` does not match the URI registered in the Dropbox App Console. The gateway must inject the same `redirect_uri` it used to build the consent URL — `connector.authorize()` reads it from `self.config["redirect_uri"]`.

### 429 `too_many_requests/`
The app or user has exceeded the Dropbox rate limit. The connector retries with `Retry-After` up to 30 s. Sustained 429 → `DropboxRateLimitError(retry_after_s)` so callers can back off intentionally.

### Health check works, sync hangs
Large accounts can have hundreds of thousands of entries; `sync()` pages with `limit=200`. If the operation needs to stream rather than buffer, switch to per-batch ingest (loop `list_folder_continue` and call `ingest_document` inline).
